title: Understanding Angular Motion
text: To find the time it takes for the wheel to turn through $$300$$ radians, we need to use the kinematic equation for angular motion. This equation relates angular displacement, initial angular velocity, angular acceleration, and time.

title: Kinematic Equation for Angular Motion
text: The kinematic equation for angular motion is: \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \omega_0 $$t$$ + \frac{1}{2} \alpha $$t^2$$ \), where \( $$\theta$$ \) is the angular displacement, \( \omega_0 \) is the initial angular velocity, \( \alpha \) is the angular acceleration, and \( $$t$$ \) is the time.

title: Initial Conditions
text: Since the wheel starts from rest, the initial angular velocity \( \omega_0 \) is $$0$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$. This simplifies the equation to \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} \alpha $$t^2$$ \).

title: Solving for Time
text: Given \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$300$$ \) rad and \( \alpha $$=$$ $$5.0$$ \) rad/s², substitute these values into the equation \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} \alpha $$t^2$$ \) and solve for \( $$t$$ \).

title: Calculate the Time
text: Substitute \( $$\theta$$ $$=$$ $$300$$ \) rad and \( \alpha $$=$$ $$5.0$$ \) rad/s² into the equation \( $$t=\sqrt{\frac{2\theta}{\alpha}}$$ \) to find \( $$t$$ \).
